publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday April 17, 2020 at 03:32

did.unittitle: Kim Stringfellow Photographs and Papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Kim Stringfellow Photographs and Papers chiefly document her projects that record the environmental and cultural history of the Southern California desert region, 1998-2017. It includes 100 inkjet print photographs related to three of her projects: Greetings from the Salton Sea , Jackrabbit Homestead , and The Mojave Project . Other material in the collection includes files, products, and electronic files that document to her projects and collaborations with others including promotional material for exhibitions and publications. Electronic files include digital photographs from her projects and of her exhibitions, as well as PDF files of publications, public relations, and press coverage, MP3 audio files for projects, video recordings made by Stringfellow and showing her at work, and copies of web sites. The collection also includes compact discs of audio tours related to her project, Jackrabbit Homestead and a group collaboration, Invisible-5 .

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Kim Stringfellow Photographs and Papers chiefly document her projects that record the environmental and cultural history of the Southern California desert region, 1998-2017. It includes 100 inkjet print photographs related to three of her projects: Greetings from the Salton Sea , Jackrabbit Homestead , and The Mojave Project . Other material in the collection includes files, products, and electronic files that document her projects and collaborations with others including promotional material for exhibitions and publications. Electronic files include digital photographs from her projects and of her exhibitions, as well as PDF files of publications, public relations, and press coverage, MP3 audio files for projects, video recordings made by Stringfellow and showing her at work, and copies of web sites. The collection also includes compact discs of audio tours related to her project, Jackrabbit Homestead and a group collaboration, Invisible-5 .

Twenty-five photographs from the project, Greetings from the Salton Sea , document the natural and built environment of the Salton Sea in California, 1998-2004. Images from the project appeared in her book, Greetings from the Salton Sea: Folly and Intervention in the Southern California Landscape, 1905-2005 (Santa Fe, New Mexico: Center for American Places, 2005). Images include views of settlements along the Salton Sea at Bombay Beach, Desert Shores, North Shore, Salton City, and Salton Sea Beach, as well as the nearby community of Niland and the mouth of the New River. Many photographs document abandoned buildings and vehicles as well as residents and visitors. A discrete group of three photographs include two portraits of Leonard Knight and a view of his visionary environment, Salvation Mountain , at Niland. Photographs of deceased wildlife in the area include a dead snow goose at the Sonny Bono Salton Sea National Wildlife Refuge and a still life of a decaying tilapia carcass.

Twenty-five photographs from the project, Jackrabbit Homestead , explore the cultural legacy in the Morongo Basin region of Southern California in 2006 to 2009 of congressional legislation, An Act to Provide for the Purchase of Public Lands for Home and Other Sites (52 Stat. 609), also known as the Small Tract Act of 1938 . The act allowed individuals to acquire small tract land property patents from the United States government for homesteads until the repeal of the act in 1976. Images from the project appeared in her book, Jackrabbit Homestead: Tracing the Small Tract Act in the Southern California Landscape, 1938-2008 (Chicago, Illinois: Center for American Places at Columbia College, 2009). Images include exterior and interior views of structures at abandoned homesteads identified by the United States land patent numbers and surnames of patentees. A group of discrete portraits show residents and artists from the region including Robert Bryan Arnett, Jeanette Susan Arnett, Christine Carraher, Eames Demetrios, Perry Hoffman, Thom Merrick, Sage Quinn-Blair, Ted Quinn, James Sibley, Laura Sibley, Doug Smith, Stephanie Smith, and Andrea Zittel.

Fifty photographs from the project, The Mojave Project , survey the physical, geological and cultural landscape of the Mojave Desert chiefly in California as well as portraits of residents and visitors to the area, 2014-2017. Views of sites in California include images at Antelope Valley, Death Valley National Park, Lucerne Valley, and Tecopa Hot Springs, as well as the communities of Adelanto, Amboy, Baker, Boron, Darwin, Joshua Tree, Lancaster, Landers, Mountain Pass, Needles, Randsburg, Shoshone, and Zzyzx. Single images document the ruins of the Llano Colony at Llano and an informal memorial to the slain McStay family at Victorville.

Activities documented in The Mojave Project include a group of photographs related to the gathering of pink halite and other minerals at Searles Lake by participants in the annual Gem-O-Rama field trip sponsored by the Searles Lake Gem and Mineral Society in October 2014, as well as a group of portraits of participants at the motor vehicle time trials of the Southern California Timing Association at El Mirage, California, in 2015, including Keturah Corbin, Jack Costella, Ralph Hudson, and Jennifer Kubo. Another discrete group of two images document the Rocketry Organization of California related to rocket launches at Lucerne Valley.

Portraits in The Mojave Project that document Mojave Desert residents include Barbara Durham and Pauline Esteves, members of the Death Valley Timbi-sha Shoshone Tribe; Drusilla Burns with her granddaughter Ashley Hemmers, members of the Fort Mojave Indian Tribe of Arizona, California & Nevada; and residents of Darwin, including Monty Brannigan, Nancy Brannigan, Hal Newell, John Hamilton. Other portraits include the singer Donovan (full name Donovan Leitch) and his wife Linda Leitch, former residents of Joshua Tree, as well as Rock Novak of Ballarat, John Woodard of Adelanto, and Ed Solski, an instructor at the National Test Pilot School in Mojave. There is also a portrait of artist Marta Becket as well as an interior view of her Amargosa Opera House at Death Valley Junction.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 7 (compact discs and USB flash drives): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Inkjet print photographs purchased from Dawson's Book Shop on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2017.
Project files and electronic files are gifts of Kim Stringfellow, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Inkjet Print Photographs, 1998-2017. II. Projects Files and Products, 2002-2017.

bioghist.head: Kim Stringfellow (born 1963)
bioghist.p: Kim Stringfellow is an artist, educator, writer, and curator based in Joshua Tree, California. Born in San Mateo, California, she received her Bachelor of Fine Arts from the Academy of Art College in San Francisco in 1988 and Master of Fine Arts in Art and Technology from the School of the Art Institute of Chicago in 2000. In 2018, Stringfellow received an honorary doctorate from Claremont Graduate University. Since 2001, she has been a professor at San Diego State University in the School of Art + Design (formerly School of Art, Design and Art History).

In her multimedia work, Stringfellow bridges cultural geography, public practice, and experimental documentary into creative, socially engaged transmedia experiences that combine writing, photography, audio, video, installation, mapping, and community engagement. She is particularly interested in the environmental repercussions of human presence and occupation within these spaces.

In addition to diverse publications and exhibitions, Stringfellow is the author of two books, Jackrabbit Homestead: Tracing the Small Tract Act in the Southern California Landscape, 1938-2008 (Chicago, Illinois: Center for American Places at Columbia College, 2009), and Jackrabbit Homestead: Tracing the Small Tract Act in the Southern California Landscape, 1938-2008 (Chicago, Illinois: Center for American Places at Columbia College, 2009).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Kim Stringfellow Photographs and Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

did.unittitle: Inkjet Print Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All prints titled, dated, and signed with an edition number by the photographs on versos. Printed on Moab Juniper Baryta Rag 305 Glossy Fine Art Inkjet Print Paper, 431 x 559 mm (17 x 22 inches).

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into three subseries: Greetings from the Salton Sea , Jackrabbit Homestead , and The Mojave Project .

title.part: Greetings from the Salton Sea

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from the project, Greetings from the Salton Sea , that document the natural and built environment of the Salton Sea in California, 1998-2004.

did.unittitle: Projects

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into two subseries: Indidividual Projects and Group Projects.

did.unittitle: Individual Projects

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four heading: Safe as Mother's Milk: The Hanford Project , Greetings from the Salton Sea , Jackrabbit Homestead , and There It Is—Take It! .

title.part: Safe as Mother's Milk: The Hanford Project

did.unittitle: Exhibition at Cornish College of the Arts, Seattle, Washington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material related to Safe as Mother's Milk: The Hanford Project , an exhibition at the Woessner Alumni Gallery, Cornish College of the Arts, November 14-December 14, 2002.

did.unittitle: Exhibition at the Tallin City Gallery, Tallin, Estonia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exhibition was part of the 12th International Symposium on Electronic Art in Tallinn, Estonia, August 17-18, 2004. Material consists of proceedings from the symposium published as International Symposium on Electronic Art, ISEA 2004: 12th International Symposium on Electronic Art (Helsinki, Finland: M-cult, 2004).

title.part: Greetings from the Salton Sea

did.unittitle: Book catalog, Center for American Places
Exhibition at John Michael Kohler Arts Center, Sheboygan, Wisconsin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material related to Kim Stringfellow: Greetings from the Salton Sea , an exhibition at the John Michael Kohler Arts Center, June 25-October 22, 2006.

title.part: Jackrabbit Homestead

did.unittitle: Press coverage of the project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press coverage of the project, Judith Salkin, "Don't Fence Me In," The Desert Sun (August 1, 2009).

did.unittitle: Press coverage of the project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press coverage of the project, which includes: Alex Schmidt, "Art in the Land," Boom: A Journal of California (Fall 2011); Christopher Michno, "Kim Stringfellow: Homestead on the Range," Artillery (December-November 2013); and Steven Biller, "Signs of Life," Art Ltd. , Desert Supplement (2013).

did.unittitle: Exhibition at University of California, Riverside

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material related to Jackrabbit Homestead: Tracing the Small Tract Act in the Southern California Landscape, 1938-2008 , an exhibition at the Culver Center for the Arts, University of California, Riverside, June 29-September 28, 2013.

did.unittitle: Exhibition at Autry National Center, Los Angeles, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material related to Kim Stringfellow's Jackrabbit Homestead , an exhibition at the Irene Helen Jones Parks Gallery of Art, Autry National Center, September 13, 2014, to August 23, 2015.

title.part: There It Is—Take It!

did.unittitle: Press coverage of the project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press coverage of the project that consists of a graphical piece derived from the project, Kim Stringfellow and Chen Design Associates, "Owens Valley & the Aqueduct," Boom: A Journal of California (Fall 2013).

did.unittitle: Group Projects
Exhibition, Paisajes Toxicos: Artistas Examinan el Ambiente , Biblioteca Nacional de Cuba José Martí, Havana, Cuba

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Catalog related to exhibition Paisajes Toxicos: Artistas Examinan el Ambiente held at the Galería El Reino de este Mundo, Biblioteca Nacional de Cuba José Martí, June 10-July 10, 2002. The exhibition included images from her project, Greetings from the Salton Sea .

did.unittitle: Exhibition, Speculative Terrain: Recent Views of the Southern California Landscape from San Diego to Santa Barbara , Sites in California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material related to exhibition Speculative Terrain: Recent Views of the Southern California Landscape from San Diego to Santa Barbara at Carnegie Art Museum, Oxnard, California, December 14, 2003, to February 11, 2004; Riverside Art Museum, Riverside, California, April 2-May 29, 2004; and Laband Art Gallery at Loyola Marymount University, Los Angeles, California, September 11-November 13, 2004. The exhibition included images from her project, Greetings from the Salton Sea .

did.unittitle: Exhibition, Tender Landscapes: Artists Respond to Human Involvement in the Natural World , Agnes Scott College, Atlanta, Georgia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material related to exhibition Tender Landscapes: Artists Respond to Human Involvement in the Natural World , Dalton Gallery, Agnes Scott College, January 29-March 5, 2004. The exhibition included images from her project, Greetings from the Salton Sea .

did.unittitle: Press coverage of Invisible-5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Press coverage of the project, Emily Eliza Scott, "Field Effects: Invisible-5 's Illumination of Peripheral Geographies," Art Journal (Winter 2010).

did.unittitle: Exhibition, American Landscape and the American Dream: An Exhibition of Works from the Comer Collection , University of Texas at Dallas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material related to exhibition, American Landscape and the American Dream: An Exhibition of Works from the Comer Collection, Arts and Technology, at the 1st Floor Gallery, University of Texas at Dallas. The exhibition included images from her project, Jackrabbit Homestead .

did.unittitle: Publication, The Collective Quarterly , Issue 6, "Mojave" (2016)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Collective Quarterly is a magazine published in Wellsburg, West Virginia, by The Collective, a media and publishing company. Each issue focuses on a place.

Stringfellow served as a primary contributor to the Mojave issue of The Collective Quarterly and wrote several articles including "Holes in the Desert," "Wasteland," "That High Desert Sound," "Greetings from the Salton Sea," and "The Jackrabbit Homestead." The issue includes images from her projects including Greetings from the Salton Sea , Jackrabbit Homestead , and The Mojave Project .

did.unittitle: Exhibition, Made in the Mojave , Museum of Art and History, Lancaster, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promotional material related to exhibition Made in the Mojave , Museum of Art and History, Lancaster, California, May 13-July 30, 2017. The exhibition included images from her project, The Mojave Project .
